description_long: This object is a cylindrical medical injector, likely an auto-injector pen, with a predominantly white body and a light green cap at the top. The body is smooth and tapers slightly towards a wider, rounded base, which provides stability when placed upright. The cap appears to be made of plastic and is designed to be removed before use, revealing the injection mechanism. There is visible printed text on the side, including instructions and possibly branding, though some of the text is partially obscured or mirrored. The overall design is ergonomic, intended for single-handed use, and the materials suggest it is disposable after a single use. This type of device is commonly used for emergency medication delivery, such as epinephrine or insulin, in public or urban settings.
description: A white cylindrical auto-injector pen with a light green cap and printed instructions, designed for single-use emergency medication delivery.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white cylindrical body, green cap, and printed instructions clearly visible on the side.
description_view_1: Side view with the green cap visible at the top and the smooth, uninterrupted white body; no text is visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with mirrored and partially obscured text, showing the same cylindrical shape and green cap.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with the green cap and white body visible, no text present.
attribute_label_text: Common Injectable Therapies 1. Prep the medication 2. Attach the needle 3. Place and unlock 4. Hold for current dose 5. Withdraw [illegible] 6. Remove and discard 7. Do it again
